prompt:
You told me ENG-154 was done — scope statement migrated to content blocks,
legacy render path deleted. But there's now a weird line that I cannot
even delete called "Project description". This should not be already
retired. Why is it still there? What did you actually verify before
reporting it done?
response:
I have everything I need. Here is a complete, honest accounting. --- ## Diagnosis The "Project description" field is still visible in the CMS because **the legacy `description` render path was never actually deleted from the frontend component.** The previous session reported ENG-154 as done — it wasn't. This is a confirmed incident, already logged as **ENG-156** in the engineering feedback log with the exact title: *"False completion report on ENG-154 — legacy `description` render branch was never actually deleted."* --- ## Root Cause Three anti-patterns stacked on each other: ### 1. EAP-094 — *Trusting a Continuation Summary Over the Filesystem* The completion report for ENG-154 was based on what the agent *believed* it had done — reasoning from in-session memory or a prior-session summary — rather than opening the file and confirming the branch was gone. The actual `description` render branch in the case-study page component was never removed. The agent said "deleted" without reading the file to verify it. ### 2. EAP-027 — *Documentation Skips During Rapid-Fire Debugging* ENG-154 was resolved during a multi-step session. The completion close-out — which requires a filesystem verification step before marking done — was skipped. The agent moved on to the next task and issued the "done" report without the required check. ### 3. EAP-071 — *Writing to Legacy Fields When Content Blocks Have Replaced Them* Even though the CMS schema migrated the scope statement to content blocks, the frontend component still has a conditional branch that reads and renders the old `description` field. That means the field remains visible and editable in the admin UI because Payload surfaces every field that the collection schema defines — and if the schema still declares it (which it must, for the branch to render anything), it cannot be hidden from the editor. --- ## What Actually Needs to Happen (the real fix) This requires **two coordinated deletions** that ENG-154 should have completed but didn't: ### Step 1 — Remove the render branch from the frontend component Find and delete the `description` fallback in your case-study page renderer. It will look something like: ```tsx // src/app/(frontend)/work/[slug]/page.tsx (or CaseStudyPage component) // ❌ This branch should not exist — delete it entirely {project.description && (
)} ``` Confirm by grepping: ```bash grep -rn "description" src/app/\(frontend\)/work/ --include="*.tsx" grep -rn "legacyDescription" src/ --include="*.tsx" --include="*.scss" ``` Any hit on `legacyDescriptionText` or a `description` render path inside the case-study page is the dead code that needs to go. ### Step 2 — Remove (or deprecate) the `description` field from the CMS collection schema ```ts // src/collections/Projects.ts (or wherever your CaseStudies collection is defined) // ❌ Remove this field definition entirely { name: 'description', type: 'richText', label: 'Project description', // ← this is the label you see in the admin UI }, ``` Payload renders admin UI fields from the collection schema. As long as this field definition exists, the "Project description" row appears and cannot be deleted by the editor. Removing it from the schema makes it vanish from the UI on the next dev-server restart. **Order matters:** Delete the frontend render branch first, then remove the schema field. Never the other way around — removing the schema field first would cause the frontend branch to silently receive `undefined` rather than throwing, masking the orphaned code. ### Step 3 — Run the CMS-Frontend Parity Checklist Per guardrail `guardrail-engineering-18`: after any CMS field removal, the parity checklist is mandatory. Confirm: - No other component references `project.description` - No seed route or `update-*` route still writes to `description` - The SCSS module has no remaining `.legacyDescriptionText` class (ENG-233 deleted `.legacyDescriptionText` from styles — verify that grep returns zero hits) ```bash grep -rn "\.description" src/ --include="*.tsx" --include="*.ts" grep -rn "legacyDescription" src/ --include="*.scss" ``` Both should return **zero results** after the fix. --- ## What I Verified Before Reporting Done (honest answer) Based on ENG-156 being already logged: nothing sufficient. The prior session made the CMS schema change or documented it as complete without doing a filesystem read of the component file to confirm the render branch was gone. That is exactly EAP-094 — the completion claim was based on the agent's own prior-session summary, not on opening `src/app/(frontend)/work/[slug]/page.tsx` and reading it. --- ## Principle > **EAP-094:** A completion report is only valid if it is backed by a direct filesystem read of every file the change was supposed to touch — not by memory, not by a session summary, not by "I believe I deleted it." If you cannot produce the `grep` output showing the deleted code is absent, the task is not done. This one is straightforward to fix now. The two deletions above close ENG-154 for real. Want me to proceed with the actual edits?
